content.problemStatement:
Given a positive integer nnn and an integer kkk such that 0≤k≤n0 \leq k \leq n0≤k≤n, find any permutation AAA of 1,2…n1, 2 \dots n1,2…n such that the number of indices for which Ai=iA_i=iAi​=i is exactly kkk. If there exists no such permutation, print −1-1−1. If there exist multiple such permutations, print any one of them.


Input Format

First line of the input contains TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, two integers n,kn, kn,k.


Output Format
For each test case, print a permutation in a single line, if a permutation with the given constraints exists. Print −1-1−1 otherwise.
constraints: Sum of n over all test cases ≤ 2·10^6
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two integers n and k (1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5, 0 ≤ k ≤ n).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line. If a permutation of {1,…,n} with exactly k fixed points exists, print any such permutation as n space‑separated integers. Otherwise print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: n=2, k=1 => k=n-1, impossible, output -1.\nTest case 2: keep position 1 fixed, rotate remaining {2,3} to get 1 3 2 which has exactly one fixed point.\nTest case 3: keep first two positions fixed, rotate {3,4} to obtain 1 2 4 3 with exactly two fixed points.

solution.approach: A permutation can have any number of fixed points k except k = n‑1. If k = n‑1 the answer is impossible because the remaining element would also be fixed. For all other k we construct a permutation: keep the first k positions unchanged (i.e., A_i = i). The remaining m = n‑k positions must contain no fixed points. When m = 0 we are done (identity). When m ≥ 2 we simply rotate these m elements by one position: for i = k+1 … n‑1 set A_i = i+1 and set A_n = k+1. This rotation is a derangement of the remaining elements. The construction runs in O(n) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, total O(Σ n) ≤ 2·10^6
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for the output array of each test case
